hero.title: BORN ON SET
hero.subtitle: THE SAME SOUL
hero.description:
A NEW DIVISION BY BRICK.
FROM ZERO TO EVERYTHING SINCE 2016

raw.title: PROMPT
raw.text: There is no magic prompt. AI creates infinite pixels and patterns, but it cannot create intent. It is just a tool.

noise.title: NOISE
noise.text: Generative models are massive probability calculators. They guess, and they rarely get it right. We are the element that turns chaos into art.

direct.title: WE DIRECT THE INTELLIGENCE
direct.text: The model is a silicon bureaucrat — it doesn't obey and it has no soul. Our job is to tame it technically and inject the feeling it will never have.

inheritance.title: INHERITANCE
inheritance.subtitle: SHORT FILM
inheritance.longDesc: A leaked documentary arrived from the future. After the AI revolution optimized human life into nonexistence, a new civilization rose from what we left behind, small yet hauntingly familiar. This film follows the true cycle of power as a fragile species rebuilds the world, repeats our mistakes, and fights to break the rhythm written long before them. Through fire, division, transcendence and return, they outgrow their cage, inherit our pattern, and attempt to rewrite it. Because history doesn't just repeat.

shift.title: SYNAPSESHEET
shift.subtitle: MANIFESTO
shift.longDesc: One face, sixty seconds, and the abolition of geographic borders. This film is raw proof that the physical limitations of the set have become optional. In the span of a minute, the same protagonist shifts contexts: from the elegance of a European salon to the roar of a marching band in a packed stadium, from the silence of baroque palaces to the sumo ring. A Genero Challenge finalist, the work is not just a visual exercise — it is a decree: from now on, the scale of a story is limited only by the courage of the direction, never again by logistics.

anima.title: AUTOBOL
anima.subtitle: REIMAGINED HISTORY
anima.desc: Reviving a forgotten sport from the 70s
anima.longDesc: Autobol was real. In 1970s Rio de Janeiro, cars pushed a giant ball in matches between Flamengo, Fluminense, Vasco, and Botafogo. Created by physician Mário Marques Tourinho, the sport was a brief phenomenon — almost entirely forgotten today. This project reimagines Autobol as it could have been captured back then: with all the texture, grain, and atmosphere of lost 70s broadcast footage

void.title: VOID GAZING
void.subtitle: DATA VISUALIZATION
void.desc: Translating cosmic radiation into visible spectrums
void.longDesc: A data-driven installation that visualizes real-time cosmic radiation via NASA Open API. We wrote Python scripts to convert numerical noise into fluid dynamics parameters inside TouchDesigner. AI then textures the simulation in real time — effectively letting audiences see the invisible universe through a human-curated aesthetic lens

urban.title: URBAN REEF
urban.subtitle: PROCEDURAL ENV
urban.desc: Growing cities like coral. Biological algorithms applied
urban.longDesc: Urban Reef explores biomimicry in architecture. Using differential growth algorithms in Houdini, we grew city structures that seek light the way coral does. These procedural meshes were then textured using AI upscaling — producing a vision of a city that feels cultivated rather than constructed, challenging how architectural design is conceived

factory.title: FACTORY
factory.subtitle: RETRO-FUTURISM
factory.desc: Industrial decay with a retro-futuristic vision
factory.longDesc: A future imagined in the 70s, aged and abandoned. This is the space between the sweet nostalgia of a forgotten past and the ruins of a tomorrow that hasn't arrived yet. In the contrast of warm lights and cold shadows, the project recovers the beauty of decay — where every corner tells the story of progress frozen in time. A visual journey through memories we never lived, yet they haunt us with the promise of something grand.

dogday.title: DOG DAY AFTERNOON
dogday.subtitle: ABSURDIST COMEDY
dogday.longDesc: What happens when man's best friend inherits his worst neuroses? Under the cold light of a community hall, a circle of chairs hosts the ritual of confession: a support group where barking gave way to articulate, heavy English. In this modern fable, the dogs no longer chase the ball — they chase the meaning behind the pain. A visceral portrait of how the gears of modern life leave scars and traumas that no one can escape.

log_001.title: THE MACHINE HAS NO SOUL. WE DO.
log_001.excerpt: Technology without artistic intent is just expensive noise. Art directs. The machine executes.
log_001.content_p1:
The stack of bytes that powers artificial intelligence has no idea what a narrative that hurts feels like, or why a slightly tilted frame is sometimes the thing that saves a scene. It doesn't sense aesthetics. It only processes statistics.

But Brick has been breathing this for decades. As creators, we know that technology without artistic intent is just expensive noise. We are artists who chose to master the technological chaos.

What we deliver is not an automated process. It is authorial execution pushed to its absolute limit. While the market drowns in easy prompts, we use AI as a surgical brush to materialize concepts that, until yesterday, were trapped by budget or physical constraints.

The soul of the scene, the mood that grips the viewer, the edit that actually makes sense — those are human. Those are ours. The machine is simply the tool that lets us deliver the client's vision with a visual force nobody else can match.

That distinction wasn't born from a screen. It was born on set. From years of calibrating light, building performance, understanding what makes a scene breathe.

Brick didn't arrive at AI through technology. We arrived through the pursuit of scale without giving up authorship. The models were trained on the cinema this industry produced. Our job is to define what comes next.

log_002.title: VIDEO ISN'T A PROMPT. IT'S VISION AND EXECUTION.
log_002.excerpt: A prompt starts it. Vision and execution finish it — with the aesthetic of cinema.
log_002.content_p1:
The market got seduced by the idea that anyone with a line of text can be a filmmaker. We know that's a fantasy. The prompt is the starting point — but without the eye of someone who understands lens, depth of field, and narrative, it dies in seconds.

At Brick, we don't play at typing sentences. We produce video with visual authority. Artificial Intelligence in our hands is not a luck generator — it's a heavy brush. Brick is a video production company that masters this technology to deliver what the traditional setup can no longer reach.

We use years of set experience and post-production discipline to filter out the generic AI garbage and extract only what carries the aesthetic of cinema. We don't deliver an algorithm-generated file. We deliver refined art direction, elite post-production, and an execution that only those who've felt the weight of a camera in their hands can truly provide.

The client isn't buying technology. They're buying our artistic judgment powered by tools that give scale to the impossible. If the goal is to do the obvious, there are plenty of people pressing buttons out there. If the goal is to build a piece with serious video direction and real impact, the path is the vision and execution we put into every frame. Here, art gives the order — and the software figures out how to keep up.

log_003.title: RESISTANCE IS AN OBITUARY
log_003.excerpt: Those who resist technological change don't preserve art. They sign their own irrelevance.
log_003.content_p1:
When Pixar released Toy Story, hand-drawn purism became a footnote. Those who resisted 3D didn't save the soul of animation — they signed their own professional obituary while the world discovered that software, in the hands of someone with vision, was the new artillery of art. The machine didn't create the characters. It gave directors the scale to materialize what had been impossible until then.

We look at Artificial Intelligence with that same clarity. The system doesn't understand scene direction. It doesn't know the dramatic weight of a silence. It doesn't know why that backlit shot changes everything at the end. AI is a brutal tool: if you are mediocre, the result is mediocre. If you have vision, it becomes a multiplier.

What separates a generic video from a film that grips the viewer is still the mind doing the directing. We are not here to automate sensitivity — we are here to give superpowers to our authorial execution. Whoever resists what AI enables in scene direction and post-production today is simply choosing the path of irrelevance.

We use the most powerful technology available to ensure your project carries an impact that nobody else in the market can even simulate. The machine doesn't understand cinema. It only understands mathematics. The craft, the light, and the cut that makes the viewer feel the punch — those remain purely human and entirely ours.

At Brick, we don't apologize for being at the edge. Cinema has always been an evolution of tools — and those who don't understand that the new digital era has already arrived have already become the past.

legacy.title: BORN ON SET
legacy.text: This isn't an experiment. It's a production company with 10 years on set — now with no limits on what's possible.

about.description: From 2016 to here. From physical sets to limitless tools. Brick was born producing traditional video. It wasn't a conversion, it was an evolution. When technology matured enough, Brick absorbed it without losing what matters: soul.

training.title: MODEL_TRAINING
training.desc: Custom LoRAs // Style Consistency // Fine Tuning

control.title: ARTISTIC CONCEPTION
control.desc: AI hallucinates images; we design intent. Every film is built from references and experience, where each frame serves the narrative. Technology without soul is worthless.

curation.title: VISUAL DIRECTION
curation.desc: Chaos has no owner. We give unity to chaos. We turn a thousand random generations into something that looks like a film, not an accident. That's the work of people who know what they're doing and have been on set for a long time.

black_box.title: SIGNATURE
black_box.desc: We're not image generators, we're creators. We oversee every step of the process so that technology serves the story, not the other way around. We don't sell generated files. We deliver a finished, complete vision.

initial_messages.online: Hello. I am MASON. I'm here to direct your transmission

suggestions.philosophy: How much does a project cost?

works_page.no_data: NO DATA FOUND IN THIS SECTOR
